Original: There are 2 [ways to win|WINNING] in AtG:[BLANK-LINE][*] Conquering the [Capital City|CAPITAL] of one of the two [Roman Empire Factions|ROME].[BLANK-LINE][*] Becoming 'Magister Militum' and taking over [Rome|ROME] from within by sending five [Clans|CLAN] to become Roman Legions.[BLANK-LINE]Note that there are two Roman [Factions|FACTION]: the Eastern and Western halves of the previously-whole-in-better-days Roman Empire, and either one can satisfy your [Victory Conditions|WINNING].[BLANK-LINE]Conquering a Roman capital will require locating it on the map and significant military preparation. Although various setbacks will befall the Romans they start off quite strong and can put up quite a fight.[BLANK-LINE]Becoming Magister Militum is more of an economic victory, as to send a [Clan|CLAN] to [Rome|ROME] not only means sending it off for a year but also providing it with expensive, late-game equipment.
Translation: AtG 中有 2 种[获胜方式|WINNING]：[BLANK-LINE][*]征服两个[罗马帝国派系|ROME]之一的[首都|CAPITAL]。[BLANK-LINE][*]成为“Magister Militum”，派出五个[氏族|CLAN]成为罗马军团，从内部接管[罗马|ROME]。[BLANK-LINE]注意，罗马有两个[派系|FACTION]：东部与西部，源自那个昔日一体的帝国；征服任一方都可以满足你的[胜利条件|WINNING]。[BLANK-LINE]征服罗马首都需要先在地图上找到它，并做好充分军事准备。罗马会遭遇各种挫折，但开局实力仍很强，会顽强抵抗。[BLANK-LINE]成为 Magister Militum 更像经济胜利：把[氏族|CLAN]派往[罗马|ROME]不仅会让他们离开一年，还需要为其提供昂贵的后期装备。
